creator: yuri.pankov@gmail.com
text: Unloading acpi_hp driver in absence of CMI (e.g., on HP Pavilion DV8, not sure if laptop doesn't have it or driver just doesn't recognize its presence, but anyway.. ) leads to "panic: sbuf_delete called with uninitialized or corrupt sbuf". We shouldn't try to free CMI-related resources if we didn't allocate them, fix logic here a bit.
